data.message: Expected: controller used to showcase what

data.given: a clinic staffer with a new owner's details
data.then: the owner is created and its detail page is shown

data.scenario: Reject an owner with invalid details
data.given: a new-owner form with missing or invalid fields
data.then: validation errors are shown and nothing is saved

data.when: a search by last name is performed
data.then: the matching, paginated owners are listed

data.when: the edit form is submitted with changes

data.scenario: Add a pet to an owner
data.when: a new pet is submitted for that owner
data.then: the pet is created under the owner

data.scenario: Reject a pet with a duplicate name for the owner
data.given: an owner who already has a pet with the same name
data.when: another pet with that name is submitted
data.then: a validation error is shown and nothing is saved

data.scenario: Book a visit for a pet
data.then: the visit is recorded against the pet

untested: the visit-form validation and controller path have no dedicated test

data.then: the vets and their specialties are listed

data.rationale: The core clinic customer data: owners and their pets, with the web controllers and repository that manage them.
The clinic's veterinarians and their specialties, exposed read-only through the vets page.
Scheduling and recording clinical visits for a pet.
Cross-cutting web endpoints: the landing page and the error-demo endpoint.
Base persistence types (id, name) reused by the domain entities.
